justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Three distinct integration patterns correctly identified and designed — SAP as polling/batch via SFTP/XML with 15-min cadence, DHL as REST/webhook with async buffer, Analytics as Kafka event stream. (2) DHL 500ms webhook constraint explicitly addressed with async ingestion buffer, thin HTTP handler completing in <200ms to leave margin. (3) DHL webhook idempotency addressed via DHL event ID deduplication. (4) SAP silent failure risk identified with specific mitigations (alert on unprocessed files after 2 poll cycles, dead-letter directory, daily reconciliation). (5) Dedicated integration layer recommended via ACL pattern per external system, with clear justification that integration logic must not live in domain services. (6) Flexibility points explicitly cover adding new integrations (e.g., FedEx) via new adapters with zero changes to core domain or existing adapters.

observations.text: Exceptional depth on the DHL webhook design — separates receipt from processing with explicit latency budget (<200ms for buffer write, leaving 300ms margin), which goes beyond the minimum requirement
SAP silent failure risk addressed with multiple layers: file tracker, alerting on unprocessed files after 2 poll cycles, dead-letter directory for unparseable files, and daily reconciliation job
Idempotency addressed comprehensively across all three integration points, not just DHL — file checksums for SAP, event IDs for DHL, eventId for domain events
Clear implementation ordering rationale: Analytics first (validates bus), DHL second (highest SLA risk), SAP last (most forgiving timing)
Pitfalls-to-avoid section explicitly warns against premature abstraction of a generic integration framework — shows mature architectural judgment

Minor: the SAP ACL is described primarily as inbound (polling SFTP for order confirmations) with outbound mentioned only parenthetically — the scenario may expect more emphasis on outbound batch file generation to SAP

agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"Integration architecture for a logistics platform connecting three external systems (SAP ERP via SFTP/XML, DHL Carrier API via REST/webhooks, Analytics via Kafka) using an event-driven backbone with dedicated Anti-Corruption Layers per integration.
